query: open https://app.example.com and click the login button

notes: dogfood phrasing but single-feature interaction, not full QA sweep

query: test the password reset feature and file a bug if it's broken
notes: 'test' could suggest qa but scoped to one feature with evidence-gathering intent

query: can you check whether the search autocomplete still fires after the refactor?
notes: post-refactor spot check, single feature, but 'check' is ambiguous

query: do a full systematic pass of the checkout flow on staging and report back
notes: near-miss: 'full systematic' signals qa, not ad-hoc browsing

query: I need to import my Arc cookies before I can test the admin dashboard
notes: near-miss: explicitly wants cookie import, not browsing yet

notes: ambiguous pre-merge guidance request

query: CEO review my plan
notes: exact trigger phrase 'CEO review'

notes: exact trigger phrase 'plan review'

query: review my plan - is this good enough to move forward?
notes: exact trigger phrase 'review my plan'

query: am I building the right thing here? here's what I'm planning to ship
notes: implies CEO-level 'right thing to build' question without trigger phrases

query: challenge my assumptions on this approach - I want to make sure we're not missing something bigger
notes: 'challenge my assumptions' implies strategic rethink

query: is this plan too small? should we be dreaming bigger with this feature
notes: scope expansion framing without exact triggers

query: what would the 10-star version of this product look like compared to what I've planned
notes: references '10-star' framing which maps to CEO review mode

query: am I even solving the right problem here
notes: ambiguous - could be eng or CEO but 'right problem' implies strategic review

query: gut check this plan for me - does the overall direction make sense
notes: 'overall direction' and gut check lean CEO but ambiguous

query: I keep second-guessing whether we should build this at all - what do you think
notes: existential build-vs-don't-build question implies CEO mode

query: review my implementation plan - I want to make sure the architecture holds up under load
notes: near-miss: 'implementation plan' + 'architecture under load' targets eng review

query: check my data model and API design in this plan - are there any edge cases I missed
notes: near-miss: data model and edge cases are eng review territory

query: go through my plan and tell me if the test coverage strategy makes sense
notes: near-miss: test coverage is an eng review concern, not CEO

query: eng review this plan
notes: exact trigger phrase 'eng review'

query: technical plan review - here's my implementation doc
notes: exact trigger phrase 'technical plan review'

query: review my engineering plan before I start building
notes: exact trigger phrase 'review my engineering plan'

query: walk me through the edge cases in this plan - I want to make sure I haven't missed anything
notes: 'edge cases' is a strong eng review signal

query: are my data models right for this? here's the schema I'm planning
notes: data model correctness is eng review territory

query: I want someone to poke holes in the architecture before I commit to it - data flow, dependencies, that kind of thing
notes: architecture + data flow language maps to eng review

query: does the sequencing in this plan make sense? I'm worried about the order of operations
notes: execution sequencing is an eng manager concern

query: does this plan have any test gaps I should address
notes: test gaps implies eng review but 'plan' could suggest CEO

query: look at this plan and tell me what could go wrong technically
notes: ambiguous - 'what could go wrong' could be CEO or eng, but 'technically' tips it

query: I think there are some performance assumptions in this plan that might not hold - can you check
notes: performance concerns are eng review but phrased ambiguously

query: is this the right problem to be solving right now given our roadmap
notes: near-miss: roadmap prioritization and 'right problem' lean CEO review

query: should we even be building this feature or is there a better way to solve the user need
notes: near-miss: build-vs-don't and user need framing is CEO territory

query: I want a critical review of this plan - are we thinking too small
notes: near-miss: 'thinking too small' maps to scope expansion mode in CEO review
